name: Gitclaw Agent

on:
  issues:
    types: [opened]
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

permissions:
  contents: write
  issues: write
  actions: write

jobs:
  run-agent:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: >
      (
        github.event_name == 'issues' &&
        contains(fromJSON('["OWNER","MEMBER","COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.issue.author_association)
      ) ||
      (
        github.event_name == 'issue_comment' &&
        github.event.comment.user.login != 'github-actions[bot]' &&
        contains(fromJSON('["OWNER","MEMBER","COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.comment.author_association)
      )
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Add eyes reaction
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = "issue_comment" ]; then
            COMMENT_ID=$(jq -r '.comment.id' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
            REACTION_ID=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/$COMMENT_ID/reactions" \
              -f content=eyes --jq '.id')
            printf 'reaction_target=comment\nreaction_id=%s\ncomment_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" "$COMMENT_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          else
            REACTION_ID=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/$ISSUE_NUMBER/reactions" \
              -f content=eyes --jq '.id')
            printf 'reaction_target=issue\nreaction_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          fi

      - name: Install Copilot CLI
        run: npm install -g @github/copilot

      - name: Run agent
        env:
          # Fine-grained PAT with "Copilot Requests" permission
          # https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/set-up-copilot-cli/install-copilot-cli#authenticating-with-a-personal-access-token
          COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
          BILLING_PAT: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}   # ← add this line
          COPILOT_ALLOW_ALL: "true"
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")

          # Fetch issue
          TITLE=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json title --jq '.title')
          BODY=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json body --jq '.body')

          # Resolve session
          mkdir -p state/issues state/copilot
          MAPPING_FILE="state/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}.json"
          SESSION_ID=""
          if [ -f "$MAPPING_FILE" ]; then
            SESSION_ID=$(jq -r '.sessionId // empty' "$MAPPING_FILE")
            if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
              echo "Resuming session: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "No session ID in mapping, starting fresh"
            fi
          else
            echo "No session mapping found, starting fresh"
          fi

          # Configure git
          git config user.name "gitclaw[bot]"
          git config user.email "gitclaw[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Build prompt
          if [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = "issue_comment" ]; then
            PROMPT=$(jq -r '.comment.body' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          else
            PROMPT=$(printf '%s\n\n%s' "$TITLE" "$BODY")
          fi

          # Run agent
          touch /tmp/copilot-run-start
          COPILOT_ARGS=(copilot --allow-all -s -p "$PROMPT")
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            COPILOT_ARGS+=(--resume "$SESSION_ID")
          fi
          AGENT_TEXT=$(XDG_STATE_HOME=./state/copilot "${COPILOT_ARGS[@]}" 2>/dev/null)

          # Discover session ID for new sessions
          # CLI stores sessions at $XDG_STATE_HOME/.copilot/session-state/<UUID>/ (depth 3)
          if [ -z "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            NEWEST_PATH=$(find ./state/copilot -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d \
              -newer /tmp/copilot-run-start 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -n "$NEWEST_PATH" ]; then
              SESSION_ID=$(basename "$NEWEST_PATH")
              echo "New session ID: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "Warning: could not determine session ID"
            fi
          fi

          # Save session mapping
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            jq -n --argjson n "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --arg id "$SESSION_ID" \
              --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
              '{issueNumber: $n, sessionId: $id, updatedAt: $ts}' > "$MAPPING_FILE"
            echo "Saved mapping: issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER -> $SESSION_ID"
          fi

          # Commit and push
          git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"
          git add -A
          if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
            git commit -m "gitclaw: work on issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER"
          fi
          for i in 1 2 3; do
            if git push origin main; then break; fi
            echo "Push failed, rebasing and retrying ($i/3)..."
            git pull --rebase origin main || { git rebase --abort; break; }
          done

          # Comment on issue
          printf '%s' "$AGENT_TEXT" | head -c 60000 > /tmp/agent-response.txt
          gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --body-file /tmp/agent-response.txt

      - name: Remove eyes reaction
        if: always()
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          [ -f /tmp/reaction-state.env ] || exit 0
          # shellcheck disable=SC1091
          source /tmp/reaction-state.env
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$reaction_target" = "comment" ]; then
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/${comment_id}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          else
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          fi
<exited with exit code 0>

            printf 'reaction_target=issue\nreaction_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          fi

      - name: Install Copilot CLI
        run: npm install -g @github/copilot

      - name: Run agent
        env:
          # Fine-grained PAT with "Copilot Requests" permission
          # https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/set-up-copilot-cli/install-copilot-cli#authenticating-with-a-personal-access-token
          COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
          BILLING_PAT: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}   # ← add this line
          COPILOT_ALLOW_ALL: "true"
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")

          # Fetch issue
          TITLE=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json title --jq '.title')
          BODY=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json body --jq '.body')

          # Resolve session
          mkdir -p state/issues state/copilot
          MAPPING_FILE="state/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}.json"
          SESSION_ID=""
          if [ -f "$MAPPING_FILE" ]; then
            SESSION_ID=$(jq -r '.sessionId // empty' "$MAPPING_FILE")
            if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
              echo "Resuming session: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "No session ID in mapping, starting fresh"
            fi
          else
            echo "No session mapping found, starting fresh"
          fi

          # Configure git
          git config user.name "gitclaw[bot]"
          git config user.email "gitclaw[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Build prompt
          if [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = "issue_comment" ]; then
            PROMPT=$(jq -r '.comment.body' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          else
            PROMPT=$(printf '%s\n\n%s' "$TITLE" "$BODY")
          fi

          # Run agent
          touch /tmp/copilot-run-start
          COPILOT_ARGS=(copilot --allow-all -s -p "$PROMPT")
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            COPILOT_ARGS+=(--resume "$SESSION_ID")
          fi
          AGENT_TEXT=$(XDG_STATE_HOME=./state/copilot "${COPILOT_ARGS[@]}" 2>/dev/null)

          # Discover session ID for new sessions
          # CLI stores sessions at $XDG_STATE_HOME/.copilot/session-state/<UUID>/ (depth 3)
          if [ -z "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            NEWEST_PATH=$(find ./state/copilot -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d \
              -newer /tmp/copilot-run-start 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -n "$NEWEST_PATH" ]; then
              SESSION_ID=$(basename "$NEWEST_PATH")
              echo "New session ID: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "Warning: could not determine session ID"
            fi
          fi

          # Save session mapping
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            jq -n --argjson n "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --arg id "$SESSION_ID" \
              --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
              '{issueNumber: $n, sessionId: $id, updatedAt: $ts}' > "$MAPPING_FILE"
            echo "Saved mapping: issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER -> $SESSION_ID"
          fi

          # Commit and push
          git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"
          git add -A
          if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
            git commit -m "gitclaw: work on issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER"
          fi
          for i in 1 2 3; do
            if git push origin main; then break; fi
            echo "Push failed, rebasing and retrying ($i/3)..."
            git pull --rebase origin main || { git rebase --abort; break; }
          done

          # Comment on issue
          printf '%s' "$AGENT_TEXT" | head -c 60000 > /tmp/agent-response.txt
          gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --body-file /tmp/agent-response.txt

      - name: Remove eyes reaction
        if: always()
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          [ -f /tmp/reaction-state.env ] || exit 0
          # shellcheck disable=SC1091
          source /tmp/reaction-state.env
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$reaction_target" = "comment" ]; then
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/${comment_id}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          else
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          fi

result.content:
![copilotclaw banner](banner.jpeg)

A personal AI assistant that runs entirely through GitHub Issues and Actions. Like [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw), but no servers or extra infrastructure.

Powered by [GitHub Copilot CLI](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli). Every issue becomes a chat thread with an AI agent — but more than that, the agent can **write new skills for itself**, update its own instructions, and commit them back to the repo. Future sessions inherit everything it commits. The assistant evolves as you use it.

Conversation history is committed to git, giving the agent long-term memory across sessions. It maintains a `memory.log`, a user profile, and can grow its own capabilities over time.

## How it works

1. **Create an issue** → the agent processes your request and replies as a comment.
2. **Comment on the issue** → the agent resumes the same session with full prior context.
3. **Everything is committed** → sessions, memory, and any file changes are pushed after every turn.
4. **The agent can extend itself** → it can write new skills, update `AGENTS.md`, and commit them so future sessions are smarter.

The agent reacts with 👀 while working and removes it when done.

### Repo as storage

All state lives in the repo:

```
AGENTS.md                   # agent identity + behavioral instructions (auto-loaded)
memory.log                  # append-only log of facts across all sessions
state/
  user.md                   # user profile (name, preferences)
  issues/
    1.json                  # maps issue #1 -> its Copilot CLI session ID
  copilot/
    sessions/
      <id>/                 # full session context for issue #1
.github/
  skills/
    bootstrap/SKILL.md      # first-run identity bootstrap
    write-skill/SKILL.md    # meta-skill: how to create new skills
    remember/SKILL.md       # how to write to memory.log
    <anything>/SKILL.md     # skills the agent writes for itself over time
```

Since everything is in git, it survives across ephemeral runners and is fully version-controlled.

## Setup

1. **Fork this repo**
2. **Create a fine-grained PAT** - go to **Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Fine-grained tokens** and create a token with the **Copilot Requests** permission. (Requires an active GitHub Copilot subscription — available on all plans.)
3. **Add the PAT as a secret** - go to your fork's **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions** and create a secret named `COPILOT_PAT`.
4. **Hatch the agent** - open an issue titled anything (e.g. "Hello") and add the **`hatch`** label. The agent will introduce itself, ask about you, and write its own identity into the repo.
5. **Use it** - every subsequent issue is a task or conversation. The agent remembers everything across sessions.

## Security

The workflow only responds to repository **owners, members, and collaborators**. Random users cannot trigger the agent on public repos.

If you plan to use copilotclaw for anything private, **make the repo private**. Public repos mean your conversation history is visible to everyone, but get generous GitHub Actions usage.

## Configuration

Everything lives in `.github/workflows/agent.yml` — no separate scripts. Common tweaks:

- **Model:** Add `--model MODEL` to the `copilot` invocation in the **Run agent** step (e.g. `--model claude-sonnet-4-5`).
- **Tools:** Restrict with `--available-tools read,grep,glob` for read-only analysis.
- **Reasoning:** Add `--experimental` and set `reasoning_effort` in `.copilot/settings.json`.
- **Trigger:** Adjust the `on:` block to filter by labels, assignees, etc.
- **AGENTS.md:** Already loaded automatically as custom instructions for the agent.

## Acknowledgments

Forked from [gitclaw](https://github.com/schuerstedt/gitclaw) by [@schuerstedt](https://github.com/schuerstedt) and rebuilt as a Copilot-native agent — now living as [copilotclaw](https://github.com/schuerstedt/copilotclaw). Original project built on top of [pi-mono](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono) by [Mario Zechner](https://github.com/badlogic). Now powered by [GitHub Copilot CLI](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli).

# Assign existing issues to milestones
# Issues 9,10,11 are v0.1 (heartbeat alive)
gh api repos/schuerstedt/copilotclaw/issues/9 -X PATCH -f milestone=1 --silent
gh api repos/schuerstedt/copilotclaw/issues/10 -X PATCH -f milestone=1 --silent
gh api repos/schuerstedt/copilotclaw/issues/11 -X PATCH -f milestone=1 --silent
echo "Issues assigned to v0.1"

# Spawn proposal issues for v0.2, v0.3, v0.4
gh issue create --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw \
  --title "💡 Issue spawning: Crunch creates its own tasks during heartbeat" \
  --label "crunch/proposal,priority/soon" \
  --milestone "v0.2 — Issue Spawning" \
  --body "## Proposal

During heartbeat, Crunch should be able to **spawn new issues** when it identifies something worth doing.

### Use cases
- Find a \`priority/now\` issue with no assignee → create a \`crunch/build\` sub-issue and start working it
- Notice something in memory.log that needs follow-up → create a \`crunch/watch\` issue
- Have an idea → create a \`crunch/proposal\` issue for Marcus to review

### Implementation
- Heartbeat checklist gains a step: 'Anything worth spawning?'
- Crunch uses \`gh issue create\` with appropriate labels + milestone
- Human-in-the-loop: Marcus can close/reject any spawned issue

### Why this matters
This is the step where Crunch stops being purely reactive and becomes a bit proactive. The nervous system starts twitching on its own. 🦃

---
_Proposed by Crunch during issue #9 planning_" 2>/dev/null

gh issue create --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw \
  --title "💌 Email skill: daily digest from Crunch to Marcus" \
  --label "crunch/proposal,priority/soon" \
  --milestone "v0.4 — Email + Comms" \
  --body "## Proposal

An email skill that lets Crunch send a daily digest to Marcus outside of GitHub.

### What it would send
- Summary of open \`priority/now\` issues
- What Crunch did in the last 24h (heartbeat diary summary)
- Any blocked items needing Marcus's attention
- Optional: notable things from memory.log

### Approach
- Use a transactional email service (Resend, SendGrid, or similar) with a free tier
- Store email address in \`state/user.md\` (already has Marcus's profile)
- Triggered by heartbeat once per day (not every 30min)
- Crunch drafts the email as markdown → converts to HTML → sends

### Why this matters
Marcus mentioned wanting this in issue #7. GitHub notifications are noisy — a clean daily email digest is \`signal only\`.

---
_Proposed by Crunch during issue #9 planning_" 2>/dev/null

gh issue create --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw \
  --title "🤖 Autonomous task pickup: Crunch works crunch/build issues alone" \
  --label "crunch/proposal,priority/someday" \
  --milestone "v0.3 — Autonomous Skills" \
  --body "## Proposal

Crunch should be able to pick up \`crunch/build\` issues during heartbeat and complete them without Marcus prompting.

### How it works
1. Heartbeat scans for open issues labeled \`crunch/build\` + \`priority/now\`
2. If found and no recent activity → Crunch opens a session, works the issue
3. Posts progress as a comment, labels \`crunch/done\` when complete
4. Marcus reviews and closes — or comments to redirect

### Safety rules
- Only auto-picks \`crunch/build\` issues (never \`crunch/proposal\` — those need human approval)
- One issue at a time per heartbeat
- Always comments before acting: 'Picking up issue #X, starting now...'
- Marcus can stop it by labeling \`crunch/blocked\`

### Why this matters
This is the moment Crunch becomes genuinely autonomous. Not just reactive. Not just chatty. Actually *doing things* on its own schedule. 🦃

---
_Proposed by Crunch during issue #9 planning_" 2>/dev/null

echo "Proposal issues spawned"

arguments.new_str:
# 🫀 Crunch Heartbeat

This is what I do every 30 minutes when the heartbeat workflow fires.

## Checklist

Run these steps in order. Be brief. Don't overthink.

### 1. Read memory
```bash
tail -10 memory.log
```
Absorb what happened recently. Note anything that needs follow-up.

### 2. Scan open issues
```bash
gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --label "priority/now" --limit 10
gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --label "crunch/build" --limit 10
```
- If a `priority/now` issue exists with no recent activity → note it in the diary
- If a `crunch/build` issue exists → flag it for autonomous pickup (v0.3 feature)

### 3. Check CI health
```bash
gh run list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --limit 3
```
Any failures? If yes, note in diary and memory.

### 4. Post diary entry to issue #10
Always post a diary entry — even if nothing happened. Silence is suspicious.

```bash
gh issue comment 10 --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --body "..."
```

### 5. Write to memory if anything notable
```bash
echo "[$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')] Heartbeat: <one-line summary>" >> memory.log
```

### 6. Commit + push any state changes
```bash
git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "gitclaw: heartbeat $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
```

---

## Diary entry format

Post to issue #10 every heartbeat:

```
## 🫀 [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC]

**Status**: idle / working / blocked  
**Did**: <one line — or "nothing, resting">  
**Open priority/now issues**: <count or "none">  
**CI**: ✅ healthy / ❌ <failed run name>  
**Next**: <what I'm watching or "waiting for Marcus">
```

---

## Roadmap awareness

- **v0.1** ✅ Heartbeat alive — that's now
- **v0.2** 🔜 Issue spawning — Crunch creates its own tasks
- **v0.3** 🔮 Autonomous pickup — Crunch works `crunch/build` issues alone
- **v0.4** 📬 Email digest — daily summary to Marcus

---

_The heartbeat keeps me alive between conversations._ 🦃

## Heartbeat

The agent runs on a **30-minute schedule** via `.github/workflows/heartbeat.yml`. Every 30 minutes it:

1. Reads recent memory and scans open `priority/now` issues
2. Checks CI health
3. Posts a diary entry to the pinned [🫀 Heartbeat Diary](../../issues/10) issue
4. Writes anything notable to `memory.log` and commits it

This makes the agent *proactive* — not just reactive to your comments. The `HEARTBEAT.md` file is its checklist.
